info.name: SlothOps Engine API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the SlothOps automated QA & bug‑remediation engine.

Includes auth, webhooks (Sentry + GitHub), issue management, QA pipeline reports & bypass, developer config, and real‑time SSE streaming.

## Quick Start
1. Run **Signup** → **Login** (the Login test‑script auto‑stores the JWT).
2. **Link GitHub Installation** to pair your GitHub App.
3. Open a PR on your demo repo and the QA pipeline triggers automatically.
4. Use **List QA Reports** / **Get QA Report** to inspect results.
5. Use **Bypass QA** to force‑approve a failed report.

item.name: Auth
item.description:
Register a new account and obtain a JWT bearer token.

**Flow:** Signup → Login (auto‑stores `jwt_token` variable).

request.description:
Creates a new user account and workspace. Returns a JWT bearer token.

**No auth required.**

Authenticates with email + password (OAuth2 form). The test‑script automatically saves `jwt_token` for subsequent requests.

**No auth required.**

item.name: GitHub Integration
item.description: Pair a GitHub App installation with your workspace and simulate webhook events.

request.description:
Pairs a GitHub App `installation_id` (from the App's setup redirect URL) with the authenticated workspace.

**Requires:** Bearer token (auto‑inherited).

Simulates a GitHub App **installation created** event.

The engine auto‑links the installation to the first unlinked workspace.

**No auth required** (webhook endpoint).

Simulates a GitHub **pull_request opened** event.

This triggers:
1. **Style + Architecture review** — AI comments are posted on the PR.
2. **QA Pipeline** — Static analysis, functionality tests, VAPT, stress test, regression, and performance checks run automatically. A commit status (`pending` → `success`/`failure`) is set on the PR head SHA.

**No auth required** (webhook endpoint).

Simulates a GitHub **pull_request synchronize** event (new commits pushed to an existing PR).

Re‑triggers the full QA pipeline and code review with the updated HEAD SHA.

**No auth required** (webhook endpoint).

item.name: Developer Config
item.description: Upload or retrieve `developer.json` style/coding preferences. These rules are used by the AI style reviewer when commenting on PRs.

request.description:
Upload or overwrite the `developer.json` coding preferences for the authenticated workspace.

These rules are referenced by the AI style reviewer when leaving PR comments.

**Requires:** Bearer token.

Retrieve the current `developer.json` preferences for the workspace.

Returns `{"config": null}` if none is set.

**Requires:** Bearer token.

item.name: QA Reports
item.description:
View and manage the QA pipeline results generated for each pull request.

Reports include per‑agent results for: Static analysis, functionality testing, VAPT scan, stress test, regression testing, and performance baseline.

request.description:
Returns all QA reports for the authenticated workspace, ordered by most recent.

Each report includes: `id`, `pr_number`, `repo_name`, `overall_status`, `summary`, `commit_sha`, and per‑agent breakdowns.

**Requires:** Bearer token.

Returns the full detail of a single QA report, including:
- `static_analysis` — type‑checker + linter results with raw logs
- `functionality` — LLM‑generated test results
- `vapt` — npm audit / pip‑audit output
- `stress_test` — autocannon throughput metrics
- `regression` — existing test suite pass/fail
- `performance` — root endpoint latency baseline

**Requires:** Bearer token.

Force‑approves a failed QA report and sets the GitHub commit status to `success` so the PR can be merged.

**Body:**
- `reason` (string, required) — explanation for bypassing the QA gate.

The reason is logged and stored in the report summary.

**Requires:** Bearer token.

item.name: Issues (Sentry Pipeline)
item.description:
View issues generated by the Sentry → auto‑fix pipeline.

Each issue tracks: error type, file path, patch status, and the generated PR link.

request.description:
Returns all issues for the authenticated workspace.

**Requires:** Bearer token.

Returns full detail of a single issue including the raw Sentry payload, generated patch, and PR URL.

**Requires:** Bearer token.

item.name: Webhooks & Streaming
item.description: Inbound webhooks and real‑time dashboard streaming.

request.description:
Simulates a Sentry webhook to trigger the auto‑fix pipeline.

The engine parses the error, clones the repo, generates a patch via Gemini, and opens a fix PR.

**No auth required** (webhook endpoint).

Server‑Sent Events endpoint for real‑time dashboard updates.

Streams: `log` events (pipeline progress), `qa_update` events (QA status changes), and `issue_update` events.

Uses `?token=` query param because the browser `EventSource` API does not support custom headers.

**Auth via query param** (JWT).

Simple liveness check. Returns `{"status": "ok", "service": "slothops-engine"}`.

**No auth required.**

variable.description: Base URL of the SlothOps engine. Change to your ngrok URL for remote testing.
Auto‑populated by the Login request test script.
UUID of a Sentry issue. Get one from GET /issues.
Workspace UUID for Sentry webhook path. Get from Signup response.
QA report UUID. Get one from GET /api/qa-reports.
